Prototype learning is inspired by cognitive psychology, which states that humans use past knowledge to judge unknown things.
Prototype-based classification methods determine the class of unknown images by their nearest neighbors in the feature space.

Prototype learning is introduced in the dense prediction task, including supervised, few-shot, and domain adaptive semantic segmentation.
Metric learning is often adopted in prototype learning to group samples of the same class together and differentiate those from different classes in the feature space.

The style difference of images is the main factor leading to the domain shift, which limits the generalization ability of the learned model.
Pioneering works have demonstrated that the feature distribution shift caused by style differences lies mainly in shallow layers of networks.
It also shows that the shallow feature distribution of networks can reflect the style information of the input image xm.
Thus, existing works always adopt the channel-wise mean and variance of the shallow feature to represent the style.
